reasoning: The effective yield for BB high yield bonds spiked dramatically in early to mid-April 2025, reaching 6.99% before crashing down rapidly to 6.24% by April 25. Prior to this April spike, yields were generally lower in Q1 2025 (around 5.98% - 6.12%). The recent strong downward momentum (dropping 45 basis points in just 4 days) combined with mean reversion towards the Q1 baseline suggests yields are likely to continue falling in the immediate term, making an increase by May 4 unlikely. Over the medium to long term (July and October), the Federal Reserve's projection of two rate cuts by the end of 2025 provides macroeconomic downward pressure on overall yield levels. Therefore, the probability of yields being higher than 6.24% remains below 50% across all horizons, with my uncertainty increasing back closer to 0.50 for the furthest date.
